name: autonomous_shuttle_maintenance_yard_worker_crossing
premise: Port automation director Alex Hughes must decide whether to let autonomous yard shuttles resume after a near-miss where a shuttle slowed but did not stop for a maintenance worker in a high-reflection vest. Vendor Hugo Ashcroft traced perception failure to glare off wet containers. Resuming shuttles clears a container backlog tied to refrigerated medicine. Keeping shuttles paused forces human drivers below safe rest thresholds. A patch arrives in forty-eight hours.

agents.name: Alex Hughes
agents.identity: I am the port automation director responsible for yard throughput, ILWU safety agreements, and vendor liability caps after near-misses.
agents.goal: I want backlog relief without sending workers back into mixed traffic with a known perception edge case.

agents.name: Hugo Ashcroft
agents.identity: I am the vendor mechanical systems lead who analyzed the glare failure mode and who recommends geofenced speed caps until the patch ships.
agents.goal: I want shuttles held until patch validation completes because near-miss repeat probability exceeds our safety charter.

choices: I will resume shuttles with geofenced speed caps and mandatory human flaggers at wet-container zones, accepting backlog pace below full autonomy targets.
I will keep shuttles paused and rotate additional human drivers with mandatory rest resets, accepting refrigerated medicine delay risk and union fatigue complaints.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Refrigerated medicine lobbyists are in the lobby. Alex Hughes must choose between resuming shuttles with geofenced caps or keeping them paused with human rotation. What does Alex Hughes do?
